preamble: The voiceless alveolar lateral affricate [tɬ] undergoes voicing and simplification to the voiced alveolar plosive [d]. This transformation involves the loss of lateral release and a change in voicing state, common in dialects undergoing phonetic reduction.

examples.note: Observed in specific Nahuatl dialects where historical lateral affricates simplified to voiced stops.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
